codeReview.summary: The billing-portal feature is well-implemented across both the Worker backend and CLI. The Worker provides a GET /billing/portal endpoint that authenticates the user, validates they have a Stripe customer ID, and creates a Stripe Customer Portal session. The CLI provides a 'spikes billing' command that fetches this URL and opens it in the default browser. The implementation follows security best practices with proper auth checks, error handling, and test coverage.

issues.description: The billing.ts handler includes handleBillingCheckout() which implements VAL-MON-006 (upgrade feature), not VAL-MON-001/002 (billing-portal feature). While this code is correct and well-implemented, it represents scope creep into a separate feature. Consider splitting into separate files for clarity.

The fetch_billing_portal() function checks status != 200 after already extracting the response body. This is a minor code clarity issue - the error response body was already consumed in the ureq::Error::Status match arm, so this check is actually unreachable for HTTP error status codes. The code still functions correctly because map_http_error handles the error cases properly.

summary: The billing-portal feature passes scrutiny review. Both the Worker endpoint (GET /billing/portal) and CLI command (spikes billing) are correctly implemented with proper authentication, error handling, and test coverage (9 comprehensive tests in billing-portal.test.ts). The feature fulfills VAL-MON-001 and VAL-MON-002 as specified. Minor non-blocking issues: (1) billing.ts includes code for the upgrade feature (VAL-MON-006) which could be split into a separate file for cleaner feature boundaries, and (2) a redundant status check in the CLI that doesn't affect functionality.
